content: I am trying to build opencpn and ran into a problem. The include file, chcnav.h, is missing. Can someone tell me what GitHub repository to add or if the file is auto-generated by a build. I am using vs code for the build.

Hi Steve. On what operating system are you compiling? Linux? Windows? Macos? There are specific instructions for each to explain how to prepare your machine before building. Have you followed those instructions for your operating system?
Where are the build instructions? I am working on Windows 11.

I was following https://www.google.com/search?q=how+do+you+use+vscode+to+develop+opencpn&oq=how+do+you+use+vscode+to+develop+opencpn&gs_lcrp=EgZjaHJvbWUyBggAEEUYOTIKCAEQABiABBiiBDIKCAIQABiABBiiBDIHCAMQABjvBTIKCAQQABiABBiiBDIKCAUQABiABBiiBDIHCAYQIRiPAjIHCAcQIRiPAtIBCTExMTA1ajBqOagCDrACAfEF-XWVLxF8NczxBfl1lS8RfDXM&client=ms-android-samsung-rvo1&sourceid=chrome-mobile&ie=UTF-8
Hello, not sure where to post this problem but I just compiled OCPN from github (v5.13.0) and the default dashboard plugin is not available anymore (not even in the plugin list). Any idea why ?
My crystal ball says you did not install it. If you read your logfile, you will see what is happening when opencpn looks for the plugins during start.

The dashboard plugin was already installed in 5.12.2. After compiling and launching 5.13 it disappeared but others installed plugins stayed (SAR, shipdriver, ais_radar, etc)

At startup it says :

The following library has encountered errors during startup:
/usr/local/lib/opencpn/libdashboard_pi.so
The library will be removed. You might want to reinstall it's associated plugin after updating the catalog

Of course I already tried to update the catalog but still missing.

Here's what the log file says :

15:18:24.610 MESSAGE plugin_loader.cpp:1495 PluginLoader: Loading PlugIn: /usr/local/lib/opencpn/libdashboard_pi.so
15:18:24.612 ERROR dlunix.cpp:120 Failed to load shared library '/usr/local/lib/opencpn/libdashboard_pi.so': /usr/local/lib/opencpn/libdashboard_pi.so: undefined symbol: _ZN8NMEA0183lsER8wxString
15:18:24.612 MESSAGE plugin_loader.cpp:1540 Ignoring blacklisted plugin libdashboard_pi
15:18:24.612 MESSAGE plugin_loader.cpp:1550 PluginLoader: Cannot load library: /usr/local/lib/opencpn/libdashboard_pi.so
This looks like bug recently introduced to the plugin API
hm... Might be related to recent problems with dashboard's bundled copy of the nmea0183 library. There are hacks in place which should be removed

Another thing: are you aware that on Linux the bundled plugins are not loaded from the correct locations unless you make make installor so and starts the installed version, usually /usr/local/bin/opencpn?

@Pavel Kalian I have sorted out the mess and is about to merge. Running into other problems in the macos legacy build: https://app.circleci.com/pipelines/github/leamas/OpenCPN/6467/workflows/dcb0990b-d2d2-4ffd-9c82-231ad8c35f55/jobs/18086

In particular:

==> Fetching mpdecimal
Warning: No available formula with the name "mpdecimal".
==> Searching for similarly named formulae and casks...
==> Formulae
mpdecimal

To install mpdecimal, run:
  brew install mpdecimal
+ :
+ brew link --overwrite python3
Error: No such keg: /opt/homebrew/Cellar/python3
+ :
+ for pkg in python3 cmake
+ brew list --versions cmake
+ brew install cmake
Warning: You are using macOS 12.

Any ideas? Will merge anyway unless there are more errors, this is as far as I understand not related.

OTOH, there is also:

[34m==>[0m [1mFetching /opt/homebrew/Library/Taps/homebrew/homebrew-cask-versions...[0m
ERROR: Repository not found.
fatal: Could not read from remote repository.

Please make sure you have the correct access rights
and the repository exists.
ERROR: Repository not found.
fatal: Could not read from remote repository.

Probably a transient error, disregard for now.

Alec Leamas said:

Another thing: are you aware that on Linux the bundled plugins are not loaded from the correct locations unless you make make installor so and starts the installed version, usually /usr/local/bin/opencpn?

Didn't know that, I always installed them with the plugin catalog..

Alec Leamas said:

hm... Might be related to recent problems with dashboard's bundled copy of the nmea0183 library. There are hacks in place which should be removed

Just recompiled the latest OCPN version on Github and the problem is solved.
The libdashboard_pi is back

Hi,

A few months ago I started this thread on the weird behavior of the "advance route waypoint on arrival only" that does not do what it says (everything explained in the post) but did not receive any response (except from TwoCanPlugIn).

I proposed a PR to add the desired behavior.
I really think this could benefit a lot of users or at least make it behave the way people think it works. Could someone take a look at it ?

Should I also create an Issue in Github so it has more visibility ?

Yes, filing a bug makes for a more sound workflow. Ideally, a PR should close a bug where we have reached a consensus about the problem and how it should be solved.

You also need to keep your PR rebased for it to be considered.

Hi Alec,
Ok, I'll open up an issue on Github and go from there.

Hi, I'm working on creating a nautical vector map of Finland. The project currently working on QGIS, but I can't find a way to export it to a vector format that's supported by OpenCPN (S-57). I was wondering if it could be feasible to export it directly OpenCPN's internal format?

Probably not. a) The task is aboout the same complex and unlike S57, where at least some tools are available, there is no tooling for it at all. b) You would cut off any other use cases and tools, which would be a shame...
Yuu migh be interested in talking to the devs behind https://freenauticalchart.net (Their tooling is at https://github.com/quantenschaum/mapping)
(deleted)

Hi there,
I do not know if I am at the right channel but I have a short question.
I have made an O plugin for a sonar panel. That project stalled because of lack of suitable devices. Now there is something on my workbench that quickly turns out to be the right solution. Now I would like to know if it is best to have a hat for an Arduino or a small achievable device to plug in your existing sounder? The device will be connected via a serial line. Ethernet is not out of reach actually, but we will see.
Thanks
Nicholas
ESP32 with Wi-Fi and firmware based on SensESP, probably, if I were doing this.

Yes, WiFi is comfortable, but using such a technology brings in a single point of failure risk.
Maybe I head for a dual/triple concept.
Just found some ESP8266 in my bread box. Will play around with them a bit.

Using a cable is also a SPOF :) Have more cables and have another APs aboard (Which the ESP32 itself can be) for redundancy. ESP32 is way more advanced than the 8266, really worth the few bucks. I don't even remember when I last touched an Arduino sice these became available. And the form factor of the minis like XIAO or the Waveshare Mini is very convenient for these applications.

At the moment the system runs fine on the ATmega so porting to an ESP should not be a big challenge. Anyhow, this makes it more flexible to implement for others.
I will take a look at the XIAO.
I just learned that the ESP32 has an ADC with up to a 20% jitter which makes it not usable for this application. I do not know if this is a hardware issue or the ESPs ADC is bad on its own.
ESP32 ADC is not brilliant, for high precision measurements something like the 16bit ADS1115 is much better solution
There are some tricks to make the internal ADC perform better (still not as good as the external one) described for example here: https://embeddedprep.com/esp32-adc-beginner-friendly-guide/
Or if you fant something faster, but 12bit, then the MCP range, the 320x

I have just got my ESP8266 throwing out UDP packages in parallel and is working successfully. So for the time being I have two options available for connecting.
It looks like I will switch to that platform as it is running satisfactorily. I am waiting for some parts and providing a usable solution comes in reach.

Quick question, according to the wiki OCPN is supposed to handle OSD sentences.
Is it still the case ? Can't get anything to be displayed in the dashboard...

Example : $GPOSD,20,A,10,M,20,M,15,30,N*6A

Kupofty said:

Quick question, according to the wiki OCPN is supposed to handle OSD sentences.
Is it still the case ? Can't get anything to be displayed in the dashboard...

Example : $GPOSD,20,A,10,M,20,M,15,30,N*6A

OpenCPN does handle the OSD message. It sends it to all plugins. What do you expect Dashboard to do with the OSD data that you could see?
As "OSD" was listed under "Dashboard Plugin recognized NMEA 0183 sentences" section, I believed some data would be displayed such as heading, course or speed

From a quick perusal, there is no NMEA 0183 OSD sentence parser in the bundled NMEA 0183 libraries, nor are OSD sentences parsed by the Dashboard plugin.
Can't believe everything you read in the documentation.

Hello,

I'm trying to build my plugin on CircleCI for build-macos-intel-legacy (all the other builds are successful)
Worked fine a few days ago. No it keeps failing at ~10min with the same error :

==> Resetting /opt/homebrew...
Switched to a new branch 'main'
branch 'main' set up to track 'origin/main'.

==> Fetching /opt/homebrew/Library/Taps/homebrew/homebrew-cask-versions...
Username for 'https://github.com':
Too long with no output (exceeded 10m0s): context deadline exceeded

Any idea what could be wrong ? First time using CircleCI so I'm no expert..

Just drop the target, Intel macs old enough not to be updatable to macOS that works with the universal binaries are pretty much all dead by now. The CI jobs for this target simply sometimes start failing and then again "magically" stop depending on what happens in the infrastructure needed for this legacy stuff. The reality is it is not worth any time spent on investigation. OpenCPN core will drop the Intel-only support after 5.14 because the infrastructure around it simply became way too fragile to live with.

Yes. Done by editing ~/.config/ciricleci.yml, just removing the last two lines referring to the target is enough.

It will removed anyway in an upcoming update to the template.

@Dave Register @Pavel Kalian

While we are on it: Isn't it time to remove the legacy build in core opencpn now in master which is heading for 5.16?

OK thanks for the info !
What about the armhf builds ?

so i find opencpn (im on 5.14 branch) is getting "stuck" underway. i stopped it in the debugger and it is inside LoadSerialPorts anyone seen this? I think the logic needs to be adapted somehow to avoid calling it too often, I'm not sure why udev is triggering it so much but its a problem forme i have to restart the program periodically

I also found since the routes were refactored in september 2025 it crashes opencpn when i delete routes sometimes.. I have backtraces. Sorry ive been out of development a while let me know should I make github issues or?

I also found since the routes were refactored in september 2025 it crashes opencpn when i delete routes sometimes.. I have backtraces. Sorry ive been out of development a while let me know should I make github issues or?

Got the same problem when deleting too many routes at once : OCPN crashes
When I start again there is often 1-2 routes remaining that I need to delete again.

I think a github issue is the more appropriate way to deal with that :wink:

metadata.xml

should I make github issues or?

Please do.

well the shipdriver plugin worked.. but my plugins that rick has been updating dont.. complains about missing
metadata.xml

Probably about a plugin using the testplugin template. There is a way to make a testplugin-based plugin to create a proper tarball, but I dont find the details -- the docs are missing. As I said, it's about somehow making a "fake" upload; the tarball is not created until the upload is done.

@Rick Gleason probably has those details.

One simple way to find out if the shipdriver or testplugin template is used is to look for the file Plugin.cmake. If it exists, we are on the shipdriver template, else testplugin.

Quick question for plugin maintainers on the opencpn cloudsmith organizarion :
Do you have "Write" or "Admin" privileges on the alpha/beta/prod repos of your plugin ?
Are you able to modify the "artifact lifecycle" or is it something fixed for all repos ?
